summary.intent: Migrate Pipelines-as-Code authentication from GitHub PAT to GitHub App credentials for manually-triggered Tekton PipelineRuns on OpenShift/Konflux cluster.
summary.outcome: Implemented solution where doozer mints GitHub App installation tokens at build time, creates uniquely-named transient secrets (art-transient-pipeline-auth-<epoch>), wires them into PipelineRuns, and automatically prunes stale secrets >24h old. PR #2707 created and rebased.

repo: PAC's secret-auto-create only works for webhook-triggered PipelineRuns, not manually-created ones
Tekton git-clone-oci-ta and prefetch-dependencies-oci-ta tasks only need credentials in first few minutes of pipeline, not throughout multi-hour builds
ART intentionally disables PAC ("pac":{"state":"disabled"}) to maintain control over PipelineRun creation
Three builder types (image, FBC, OLM) all call start_pipeline_run_for_image_build with same parameter pattern
github_auth.py already had get_github_app_token_from_env() with caching and multi-org support

code.finding: git_auth_secret parameter already existed with default 'pipelines-as-code-secret' - just needed to be wired through the call chain
PipelineRun template uses {{ git_auth_secret }} variable and mounts workspace as basic-auth type, consumed by clone-repository and prefetch-dependencies tasks only
KonfluxClient already imported CoreV1Api and had k8s client access for secret creation/deletion

workflow: GitHub App private keys cannot be used directly by git - require JWT-to-token exchange via GitHub API first
GitHub App installation tokens expire after 1 hour, but git-clone only needs them for initial checkout
Kubernetes basic-auth secrets expect username/password fields (username: x-access-token, password: <token>)
Creating unique per-invocation secrets avoids contention vs patching a shared secret with 200+ concurrent builds
Idempotent cleanup pattern: catch ApiException 404 when deleting secrets that may already be deleted by concurrent jobs
Timestamped secret names (art-transient-pipeline-auth-<epoch>) enable stale secret identification and cleanup

friction: Initial confusion about whether PAC secret-auto-create could work for manually-triggered PipelineRuns
Plan mode restriction blocked code execution when user said 'lets execute' - required explicit mode switch
Pre-commit hook required dangerouslyDisableSandbox on first commit attempt
asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecation warning required test helper refactor to use asyncio.run()
open_items: No monitoring/alerting on git auth secret creation or cleanup failures
Cleanup only happens after builds complete - if doozer crashes, stale secrets remain until next job (max 24h)
24-hour retention window is hardcoded in cleanup_stale_git_auth_secrets - could be made configurable
No validation that GitHub App has access to required repositories before creating secrets
No metrics/logging for tracking transient secret lifecycle (created count, cleaned count per job)
